# Results [PLACEHOLDER]
<!-- [PLACEHOLDER] -->

This chapter reports the evidence for the thesis-facing experiment:
the TD3 agent trained on high-frequency AAPL order-book-derived features under
the state-space design defined in Chapters 4 and 5. It is organized in three
parts: statistical validation of repeated-run variability, robustness
assessment under nearby design changes, and final performance evaluation on the
held-out split.

## Benchmark Strategies [PLACEHOLDER]
<!-- [PLACEHOLDER] -->

Performance is assessed relative to five benchmark strategies. These
benchmarks span passive exposure, execution-style references, and a
stochastic baseline, together providing a comparison surface for
the directional TD3 agent.

**Buy-and-hold** maintains a constant fully long position ($a_t = 1.0$)
throughout the evaluation period, capturing the full market return of the
asset. It is the primary economic benchmark: any active strategy that fails to
outperform buy-and-hold on a risk-adjusted basis has not demonstrated useful
directional skill beyond passive exposure.

**Short-and-hold** maintains a constant fully short position ($a_t = -1.0$).
Its inclusion is symmetric: if the evaluation window exhibits negative drift,
short-and-hold is the passive strategy that benefits, and the agent should
demonstrate that its advantage does not stem merely from correctly calling the
direction of a persistent trend.

**TWAP (Time-Weighted Average Price)** distributes position changes uniformly
across time. **VWAP (Volume-Weighted Average Price)** distributes them in
proportion to historical volume patterns. Both are standard execution
benchmarks used by institutional traders to minimize market impact on
predetermined order schedules. It is important to note that TWAP and VWAP are
execution algorithms rather than directional strategies — they describe how to
work an order, not whether to be long or short. Their inclusion reflects
standard practice in trading-system evaluation; however, outperforming them
has a different interpretation than beating buy-and-hold, and they
serve as lower-bar references rather than directional competitors.

**Random** selects actions uniformly from $[-1, 1]$ at each step without
reference to market state. Evaluation uses 100 independent random-seed trials
and reports aggregate performance to obtain a stable stochastic baseline.
Failure to consistently outperform a random policy would indicate that the
agent has not learned a meaningful relationship between observations and
actions.

## Evaluation Protocol [PLACEHOLDER]
<!-- [PLACEHOLDER] -->

To keep the rendered thesis aligned with the recorded experiments, tables and
figures are loaded from exported result snapshots generated by the experiment
tracking workflow. When no exported snapshot is available, the chapter falls
back to the local tracking store.

Two result views are used throughout:

- a monitoring view of the latest active run, when training is still in progress
- an evaluation view of the latest finished run with test-set artifacts

```{python}
#| label: exp7_setup
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd

from thesis_mlflow_results import load_experiment_snapshot, runs_overview_table

EXPERIMENT_NAME = "TRADINGENV_TD3_AAPL_HFT_STATE_SPACE"
snapshot = load_experiment_snapshot(EXPERIMENT_NAME)
runs_df = runs_overview_table(EXPERIMENT_NAME)
```

## Experiment Snapshot [PLACEHOLDER]
<!-- [PLACEHOLDER] -->

```{python}
#| echo: false

if runs_df.empty:
    print(f"No runs found for experiment: {EXPERIMENT_NAME}")
else:
    view = runs_df.copy()
    for col in ["start_time", "end_time"]:
        if col in view.columns:
            view[col] = view[col].astype(str).str.replace("NaT", "", regex=False)
    display(view)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false

def _series_to_frame(series, title):
    if series is None or len(series) == 0:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["Metric", "Value"])
    items = []
    for k in sorted(series.index):
        v = series[k]
        try:
            items.append((k, f"{float(v):.6f}"))
        except Exception:
            items.append((k, str(v)))
    df = pd.DataFrame(items, columns=["Metric", "Value"])
    print(title)
    display(df)

if snapshot.latest_running:
    _series_to_frame(snapshot.latest_running.get("latest_metrics"), "Latest active run metrics (monitoring snapshot)")
else:
    print("No active run detected for the selected experiment.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from PIL import Image

run_for_plot = snapshot.latest_running or snapshot.latest_finished
plot_path = None if run_for_plot is None else run_for_plot.get("evaluation_plots", {}).get("merged_comparison")

if plot_path and Path(plot_path).exists():
    img = Image.open(plot_path)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
    ax.imshow(img)
    ax.axis("off")
    ax.set_title("Latest available evaluation comparison plot")
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No evaluation comparison plot artifact available yet.")
```
